title: Cheap apartments for rent in Lakewood, OH
metaTitle: Cheap Apartments for Rent in Lakewood, OH
metaDescription: Searching for cheap apartments for rent in Lakewood, Ohio? See Cleveland-area rent benchmarks, transit, and Section 8 facts, then book a free showing nearby.
primaryKeyword: cheap apartments for rent Lakewood ohio
answerBox: Lakewood, Ohio is a Lake Erie suburb immediately west of Cleveland's West Side, bordering the Cudell and Edgewater neighborhoods. Greater Cleveland apartment rents run roughly $1,060–$1,350/month by size per Zumper (July 2026). Rent Finder Cleveland doesn't currently work with units inside Lakewood but manages Section 8-friendly homes just across the border in Cleveland's 44102 ZIP.

body: Lakewood sits directly on Lake Erie, immediately west of Cleveland's West Side, sharing a border with Cleveland's lakefront neighborhoods. It's one of Cuyahoga County's West Side suburbs, and its housing stock leans toward older apartment buildings and converted multi-family homes rather than new-construction complexes.

There's no independently published Lakewood-only rent index, so the closest reliable benchmark for renters comparing prices is the broader Greater Cleveland metro-area data below. Actual Lakewood pricing depends heavily on the specific building, unit condition, and whether it's a classic pre-war walk-up or a newer conversion.

sections.h2: How much do apartments cost near Lakewood?
body: Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts the Greater Cleveland median asking rent at $1,250/month overall, with the size breakdown below. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which tracks larger professionally managed apartment communities, shows notably higher averages for the same bedroom counts — the gap reflects the mix of older, smaller buildings versus newer managed properties. Both sources agree the Cleveland area remains well below the national median.

sections.h2: Lakewood's location and nearby Cleveland neighborhoods
body: Lakewood borders Cleveland's West Side lakefront neighborhoods — **Cudell** and **Edgewater** — both of which sit along Detroit Avenue and the Lake Erie shoreline, with Edgewater home to Edgewater Park, part of the Cleveland Metroparks Lakefront system. Because Lakewood sits on Cleveland's west side of the Cuyahoga River, it typically sees less lake-effect snowfall each winter than communities on the city's east side.
GCRTA, the regional transit authority, runs bus service throughout Lakewood connecting to Downtown Cleveland and the Tower City rail hub, where the Red, Blue, Green, and Waterfront lines converge.

items: **Position:** West side, on Lake Erie, directly bordering Cleveland
**Transit:** GCRTA bus routes to Downtown/Tower City

sections.h2: Does Rent Finder Cleveland have apartments in Lakewood?
body: We work with 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland, concentrated on the city's East and Southeast sides, with a smaller presence on the West side — including the **Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (ZIP 44102)** neighborhood that directly borders Lakewood — but we don't currently have listings inside Lakewood itself.

If you're open to that adjacent Cleveland neighborhood or others nearby, [book a free showing](/p/book-showing) and tell our team your bedroom count and budget. You can also browse our [Cleveland apartments guide](/apartments-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) or the current [Lakewood apartment listings roundup](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/apartments-for-rent-lakewood-ohio/) for other options in the area.

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher-friendly apartments near Lakewood
body: Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law, so whether a landlord accepts a Housing Choice Voucher depends on local ordinance or the individual owner's policy. As of July 2026, the reported Cuyahoga County suburbs with a local voucher-holder protection ordinance are Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale — Lakewood is not currently on that list, so acceptance there depends on the individual landlord; renters should verify directly.

Every rental home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, including our homes near Lakewood in the 44102 ZIP. Vouchers in Cuyahoga County are administered by the **Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)**; our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) covers applying, waitlists, and inspections.

sections.h2: Tips for finding a cheap apartment near Lakewood
body: When comparing prices, check both Zumper's median-asking figures and RentCafe's professionally-managed averages — Zumper's own reporting notes that 2-bedroom asking rents can sometimes sit at or below 1-bedroom rents simply because of which units happen to be listed that month, not a pricing error. Widening your search a few blocks east into Cleveland's Cudell and Detroit-Shoreway neighborhoods can open up more voucher-friendly options at similar price points.

If a Housing Choice Voucher is part of your plan, start the CMHA application in parallel with your apartment search — preliminary intake runs year-round with no closing date, but placement is by random lottery and can take anywhere from a few days to several months.

faq.q: Are there cheap apartments for rent in Lakewood, Ohio?
faq.a: Lakewood's rental stock is mostly older apartment buildings and converted multi-family homes along its Lake Erie West Side location. There's no Lakewood-only rent index, but Greater Cleveland metro rents run roughly $1,060–$1,350/month by size per Zumper's July 2026 report; exact Lakewood pricing depends on the specific building.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with apartments in Lakewood?
faq.a: Not currently. Our 90+ rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's East and Southeast side, with a smaller West Side presence in the adjoining Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway ZIP (44102) that borders Lakewood. Book a free showing to see what's currently available nearby.

faq.q: Does Lakewood accept Section 8 housing vouchers?
faq.a: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers. Lakewood is not currently among the reported Cuyahoga County suburbs (Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, Linndale) with a local source-of-income protection ordinance, so acceptance depends on the individual landlord.
Lakewood borders Cleveland's West Side lakefront neighborhoods, including Cudell and Edgewater, both along Detroit Avenue near Lake Erie, with Detroit-Shoreway just beyond.

faq.q: How do I get from Lakewood to Downtown Cleveland?
faq.a: GCRTA bus routes run through Lakewood into Downtown Cleveland, connecting at the Tower City rail hub, where the Red, Blue, Green, and Waterfront rail lines all converge for onward travel.
